instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `do-pairs` that walks a list two elements at a time, binding each pair to user-named symbols. The walking pointer is held in a hidden gensym so the list expression is evaluated once.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro do-pairs ((a b list) &body body)
  (let ((g (gensym)))
    `(let ((,g ,list))
       (loop while ,g
             do (let ((,a (pop ,g))
                      (,b (pop ,g)))
                  ,@body)))))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 LST))
            (LOOP WHILE #:G1
                  DO (LET ((K (POP #:G1)) (V (POP #:G1)))
                       (PRINT (CONS K V)))))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 LST))
            (LOOP WHILE #:G112
                  DO (LET ((K (POP #:G112)) (V (POP #:G112)))
                       (PRINT (CONS K V)))))
